body.title: CLOSED: twm_leap: ? placeholder syntax caused Postgres errors on boot
body.result: Fixed in 0236d93a: replaced ? with %s at twm_leap.py:89,109. Part of 11-file SQLite placeholder sweep.
body.description:
twm_leap.py used SQLite-style ? parameter placeholders in two execute() calls (lines 89, 109), causing Postgres to throw 'syntax error at or near AND' on every twm_push since the leap sweep was introduced. Igor logged 'twm_leap sweep failed (non-fatal)' approximately every 600ms during boot. Fixed in commit 0236d93a as part of the broader SQLite→%s sweep across 11 files.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/twm_leap.py (lines 89, 109)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (CLAUDE.md hard rule)
**Scope boundary:** placeholder syntax fix only — no logic change; leap semantics unchanged
**Test plan:** no dedicated test added (sweep commit); twm_leap covered by integration path via cortex.twm_push
body.done_summary: Fixed in 0236d93a: replaced ? with %s at twm_leap.py:89 (SELECT) and :109 (UPDATE). Error was 'syntax error at or near AND' because Postgres received literal ? characters. Covered in the 11-file, 51-site SQLite placeholder sweep.
